message.content:
Your architecture was attacked by independent skeptics on three lenses: (1) does it open a HALLUCINATION hole, (2) can the RACE judge actually SEE it, (3) is it above the noise floor / is it just the failed STEP-3 insight directive again.

RESULTS:
[
 {
  "change": "Light the dark synthesis lane (zero new code)",
  "id": "C1",
  "survives": false,
  "refuted_votes": "3/3",
  "objections": [
   {
    "refuted": true,
    "reasoning": "REFUTED — the change opens a live hallucination hole, and I reproduced it by executing the real gate code with exactly the three proposed env flags.

## 1. The safety contract in the proposal DOES NOT EXIST IN THE CODEBASE

`grep -rn "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER|inference_tier|INFERENCE_OK" src/` returns **zero hits**. The entire "TIER I" apparatus the proposal leans on — the 6-condition admission test, the "SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt", the `soft_warning='inference_tier'` audit tag, "CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal", "every MECHANICAL leg already passed" — is vapor. The change is explicitly *env-only, zero new code*, so none of it ships. What actually ships is TIER I-S, i.e. the code at `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:689-720`. Every faithfulness assurance in the proposal must be read against that code alone.

## 2. In BASKET MODE THE JUDGE IS COMPUTED, PAID FOR, AND THEN IGNORED

`analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:689-705`:

```python
if basket_mode_enabled():
    _bspan = basket_spans[i]
    grounded = bool(_bspan.strip()) and _span_grounds_sentence(base, _bspan)
else:
    grounded = (has_span and supported.get(i, False)
                and _span_grounds_sentence(base, spans[i])
                and _frozen_engine_verifies_sentence(base, cited_rows_per_sentence[i]))
```

Turning on `PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_MODE=1` **deletes two of the three legs**: `supported[i]` (the Sentinel groundedness judge) and `_frozen_engine_verifies_sentence` (the `verify_sentence_provenance` / strict_verify / NLI re-pass). The judge futures still run at :635-658 (real money, real latency) and the result is discarded. The sole surviving gate is `_span_grounds_sentence` (:138-152): **(a) every decimal in the sentence is a substring-set member of the UNION of cited full texts, (b) >=2 shared content words.** That is a bag-of-tokens check. It has no notion of direction, negation, polarity, subject, or attribution. There is *no* contradiction check anywhere in this path — the proposal's "WHAT STILL GETS KILLED... any sentence contradicted by its spans" is simply false for the lane it is turning on.

## 3. CONCRETE PATHS TO A FALSE CLAIM IN THE REPORT (executed, not theorized)

With `PROMOTE_GROUNDED=1 BASKET_MODE=1 DISCLOSED_KEEP=1` and a `judge_fn` that returns UNSUPPORTED for *every* sentence (simulating Sentinel calling it contradicted):

**(a) Polarity inversion of a real cited finding — PROMOTED as verified.**
Evidence row: Brynjolfsson, "generative AI *increased* worker productivity by 14%, largest gains among novice workers."
Input sentence: `"The field experiment shows that access to the AI assistant decreased worker productivity by 14 percent, with the largest losses among novice workers [1]."`
Output: kept, `synthesis_deviation_promoted_count: 1`, tagged
`[confidence: moderate — moderate confidence — verified against the cited source]`.
"14" is in the span; content overlap is huge; done. The pipeline **asserts it verified a claim that is the exact opposite of its source.** This is worse than a silent hallucination — it is a false verification record.

**(b) Cross-source number transplant — PROMOTED as verified.**
`"Eloundou and colleagues estimate that 14% of the U.S. workforce will lose their jobs to LLM exposure [1][2]."` — the 14 is Brynjolfsson's *productivity gain* from [1], welded onto Eloundou's *task-exposure* work in [2], reframed as job loss. Passes because the number check is against the **UNION** of the cited spans (`_resolve_sentence_span(..., full_text=True)`), so any number from any cited source may be attached to any other source's subject. Promoted, "verified against the cited source." `PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_FULLTEXT=1` (default ON) makes this strictly worse: the bigger the unioned text, the more numbers are free to splice.

**(c) Fabricated organizational attribution — KEPT under disclosed-keep.**
`"Goldman Sachs research suggests the displacement mechanism may already be visible in hiring freezes."` — uncited, zero evidence, no span, no judge. Kept. `_is_disclosable_interpretation` (:264-302) only guards multi-author patterns (`_AUTHOR_ATTR_RE` = `Surname et al` / `Surname and Surname`); a **single name or an organization** ("Goldman Sachs", "the WEF", "MIT", "the FDA") is not matched at all, and there are no cited rows to check it against anyway. The proposal's claim that "any sentence introducing a ... named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources" is killed is false on its face for a lane whose defining property is *having no cited sources*.

**(d) Sweeping categorical + fabricated causal mechanism — KEPT.**
`"Generative AI will likely replace all lawyers within the decade..."` kept (hedge cue `likely`; `lawyers` is not in `_QUANT_DOMAIN`). `"The mechanism appears to be that firms quietly reclassify displaced staff as contractors..."` kept (cue `appears`). The hedge-cue list at :219-226 includes `may|might|could|likely|appears|suggest*|remain*|requir*|represent*|reflect*` — that is a filter essentially every LLM sentence passes. It filters *tone*, not *truth*.

## 4. PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN (default ON) ERASES THE ONLY READER-FACING WARNING

`analyst_synthesis.py:604-637` strips every inline `[confidence:…]` marker and replaces them with one aggregate note. Running the full chain end-to-end, the reader receives:

> _Confidence labels for this interpretive section: 1 moderate, 2 no-source-found. …_
>
> The field experiment shows that access to the AI assistant **decreased** worker productivity by 14 percent, with the largest losses among novice workers **[1]**. Goldman Sachs research suggests… The mechanism appears to be that firms quietly reclassify displaced staff as contractors.

The inverted claim ships as **bare prose carrying a [1] citation** — indistinguishable from a verified body claim — and the reader has no way to map "1 moderate, 2 no-source-found" back onto specific sentences. The proposal treats `PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN` as a cosmetic ally ("markers are stripped and summarized"); it is in fact the component that converts a labeled unverified claim into an unlabeled one. And the *same proposal* then softens `ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE` (:64-69) from "not individually span-verified; not audit-grade claims" to "the authors' interpretive synthesis" — removing the last honest signal precisely because it reads as "a self-indictment". That is the tell: the mechanism being suppressed is the disclosure that made the lane defensible, and it is being suppressed *to score better on the dimension it is warning about*.

## 5. THE AUDIT-TRAIL CLAIM IS ALSO FALSE

"every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection" — at :746-753 only `dropped` sentences are logged with their text. PROMOTED and DISCLOSED keeps are **counted only** (and `synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count` isn't even initialized in the telemetry dict at :569-574, so it's absent when zero). There is no enumeration of what interpretation shipped.

## 6. WHY THE LANE IS OFF THREE FLAGS DEEP

The proposal frames the triple gate as accidental darkness. The emitter fail-close at `analyst_synthesis.py:685-692` is a *deliberate* P0 guard, and its own comment says the ungated posture "was banned." The lane isn't dark because someone forgot; it is dark because turning it on removes the entailment engine from the longest, most confident block in the report. The +1.0 to +2.0 insight upside is speculative; the downside is a directly-contradicted, [N]-cited, marker-stripped sentence stamped "verified against the cited source" — which vaporizes POLARIS's entire moat in a single sentence of a single report.

Repro: `/tmp/hole.py`, `/tmp/hole2.py` (PYTHONPATH=/home/polaris/wt/flywheel).",
    "fix": "If this lane is to be lit at all, the flag flip alone is unshippable. Minimum required (this is NOT zero-code):

1. **Restore the judge in basket mode.** `analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:698` must become `grounded = bool(_bspan.strip()) and _span_grounds_sentence(base, _bspan) and supported.get(i, False)`. Basket mode may legitimately relax the *verbatim/entailment* leg (the 79/81 over-drop), but dropping the groundedness judge means nothing checks polarity or contradiction. As written, the judge result is computed and thrown away — that is almost certainly an unintentional bug, independent of this proposal.

2. **Add a directional/contradiction leg.** A CONTRADICTED verdict from the Sentinel (or an NLI contradiction against the union) must be unconditionally fatal in basket mode. Today `_span_grounds_sentence` cannot distinguish "increased 14%" from "decreased 14%".

3. **Bind numbers to their source, not the union.** Require each decimal to ground in the span of a source cited *in that clause*, or at minimum require the number AND its adjacent subject noun to co-occur within one cited row's text — otherwise cross-source number transplant is free.

4. **Do NOT rewrite ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE while admitting more unverified content.** If anything the disclosure must get stronger. And per-sentence labels must survive render: `PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN` must not strip the `no-source-found` marker from disclosed-keep sentences (aggregate counts are not a disclosure).

5. **Extend the entity guard beyond `Surname et al` / `Surname and Surname`** to single-name and organizational attributions before any uncited sentence may name an actor.

6. **Enumerate promoted + disclosed sentences in the audit log**, not just drops, so the claim of post-hoc inspectability is true.

7. Land the actual `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER` code if the two-tier contract is the intent — right now the contract is describing software that does not exist."
   },
   {
    "refuted": true,
    "reasoning": "REFUTED — and not on judgment, on a mechanical fact: the analyst-synthesis text never reaches the RACE-scored artifact on the path we actually run.

## 1. The kill shot: the scored composer never renders the section

The report that gets RACE-scored every night is built by `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py` (confirmed: `overnight_supervisor.sh:9,26,30` pgrep/pkill on `python.*compose_agentic`; every `outputs/rank7..rank13_*_compose/` dir carries that script's `compose_summary.json`).

Its report assembly, lines 652-668:

```python
bodies: list[str] = []
for sr in multi.sections:
    if sr.dropped_due_to_failure or not sr.verified_text: continue
    bodies.append(f"## {sr.title}\n\n{sr.verified_text}")
sections_concat = "\n\n".join(bodies)
if getattr(multi, "limitations_text", ""):
    sections_concat += f"\n\n## Limitations\n\n{multi.limitations_text}"
...
final_report = (f"# {title}\n\n{intro}\n\n{sections_concat}{biblio_section}")
(run_dir / "report.md").write_text(final_report, encoding="utf-8")
```

report.md = title + fixed intro + verified section text + Limitations + References. `grep -c -i "analyst" scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py` → **0**. The field `MultiSectionResult.analyst_synthesis_text` (multi_section_generator.py:1431, populated at :12268) is read by exactly ONE renderer in the tree: `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15622-15629` — the clinical sweep runner, which is NOT the flywheel/RACE path. Confirmed by artifact: `outputs/rank12_tierfirst_compose/report.md` headings are Introduction → 6 thematic bodies → Cross-Study Synthesis → Conclusions → Additional Corroborated Findings → Limitations → References. No Analyst Synthesis. Ever.

So the proposed "env-only arm flip, zero new code" produces a **byte-identical report.md**. Expected RACE delta: exactly 0.0000. The claim "+1.0 to +2.0 overall" is not merely unproven — it is mechanically impossible under the stated change.

## 2. Its actual effects are all negative

The flip is not a no-op; it is a *cost-only* op.
- **Today the lane is free, not "running and emitting nothing."** The emitter fail-close (analyst_synthesis.py:685-695) returns `("",0,0)` **before** `OpenRouterClient` is constructed. The premise "the layer runs every night and emits nothing" is false — it short-circuits pre-call.
- Flipping PROMOTE_GROUNDED=1 **arms** a DeepSeek reasoning-first call with `reasoning_max_tokens=32768` + `max_tokens=PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS=24000` per render, **plus** a per-sentence Sentinel (MiniMax-M2) groundedness judge over ~100-150 synthesis sentences (`screen_synthesis_against_baskets`, 8-way parallel, 60s deadline each) — i.e. ~100 extra LLM calls and several minutes per render, in a loop where render speed is already a tracked problem — for text that is then discarded.
- It **corrupts the length instrument**: multi_section_generator.py:11986-11988 adds the synthesis words/tokens to `total_words`/`total_in_tok`, and compose_summary.json reports `total_words`. The flywheel is currently reasoning about a length-vs-density trade-off (Rank7/9/10). This arm would inflate `total_words` by 1500-3000 while report.md is unchanged — silently poisoning the exact metric the next decisions depend on.

## 3. Even if someone patches the composer (~8 lines), it still loses

- **The gate structurally guarantees waffle.** DISCLOSED_KEEP admits an uncited sentence only if `_is_disclosable_interpretation` (deviation_check.py:264-302) passes: **no decimal, no "percent", no quantitative claim, no un-pooled attribution, and a mandatory hedge cue** (`_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE`: suggest/may/might/could/broadly/typically/appears/likely/plausibly/…). The new admitted content class is therefore *by construction* number-free, specificity-free, hedge-saturated prose. RACE strips citations and grades Insight on non-obvious, specific, supported claims. This is the *worst possible* content for the target dimension — and it is a stronger version of the insight-directive failure, not an escape from it: the directive at least pushed *quantification* into synthesis; this admits the residue quantification is forbidden from.
- **This judge is demonstrably concise-preferring.** Team's own measurements (OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md:337): "the lean/top-24 cap that lifted RACE (0.4245) WITHHOLDS most sources — pleases the concise-preferring RACE judge." Climb was lean 2910w=0.4058 > depth 4028w=0.3982 > floor 3181w=0.3959. rank12 is already **7829 words**. Adding 1500-3000 words of hedged commentary is the direction the evidence says loses. The proposal concedes this (failure mode (a)) and then defers the fix to a *different* change (C6) that is not part of C1 — i.e. C1 as scoped is admitted-negative-EV by its own author.
- **Redundancy penalty.** The report already ships `## Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions` and `## Conclusions and Research Gaps`. The analyst prompt (analyst_synthesis.py:110-118) requests "Mechanism interpretation / Clinical implications / Comparative considerations / Open questions" — a second, unverified, hedgier restatement of sections that already exist. Both prior A/B arms moved all four RACE dims together; Instruction-Following and Readability would take this hit too.
- **The PG_SYNTH_DISCLOSURE_SCHOLARLY rewrite does not do what it claims.** It edits `ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE` (analyst_synthesis.py:64-69) only. But in PROMOTE mode every kept sentence gets a BUCKET_MODERATE `[confidence:…]` marker, and `_render_clean_synthesis` (analyst_synthesis.py:~628-634, PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN default **ON**) then emits into the prose: *"Confidence labels for this interpretive section: N moderate… **the whole section is interpretive and not individually span-verified**."* The self-indictment the proposal exists to remove ships anyway, from a paragraph the proposal never touches.

## 4. Detectability

Claimed effect +0.010-0.020 overall. Measured RACE judge variance is ~0.016 (champion 0.4447 → re-score 0.4291; n=3 step3 sd 0.0036 but between-run/report spread far larger — the prior insight A/B moved 0.035 purely from a 588-word outline-nondeterminism gap). Even the optimistic claim sits inside the noise floor of a single paired live run, so this arm is not falsifiable in one night on the current harness.

**Verdict: refuted.** As specified (env-only), it changes nothing scored and costs real money and wall-clock. As it would have to be implemented (composer patch), it appends hedge-mandatory, number-forbidden prose to an already-over-length report for a judge measured to reward concision, in a section that duplicates two existing sections, while still printing "not individually span-verified" into the graded body.",
    "fix": "If anyone still wants to test this lane, the change is NOT env-only and must be re-scoped honestly:

1. REQUIRED CODE (the proposal's blocker): patch /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:658 to render the section, e.g. immediately before the Limitations append:
   ```python
   if getattr(multi, "analyst_synthesis_text", ""):
       from src.polaris_graph.generator.analyst_synthesis import ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE
       sections_concat += (f"\n\n## Analyst Synthesis\n\n*{ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE}*\n\n"
                           f"{multi.analyst_synthesis_text}")
   ```
   Without this, the three env flags provably cannot move RACE.

2. ALSO REQUIRED if the "no self-indictment" goal is to be met: PG_SYNTH_DISCLOSURE_SCHOLARLY must rewrite BOTH the disclosure constant (analyst_synthesis.py:64-69) AND the render-clean confidence note (analyst_synthesis.py:~628-634), which independently prints "the whole section is interpretive and not individually span-verified" into the graded body. Alternatively set PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN=0 and drop the markers entirely.

3. CHEAP PRE-TEST BEFORE SPENDING A RENDER (do this first): the lane's output is deterministic-ish given a fixed corpus. Run the composer once with the flags on and PG_STOP short-circuited, dump `multi.analyst_synthesis_text` to a file, and manually paste it into a copy of outputs/rank12_tierfirst_compose/report.md. RACE-score paste-vs-original as a matched pair. Zero pipeline risk, one judge call, and it answers "would the judge SEE and REWARD this" for ~$1 instead of a full render.

4. GATE THE ARM ON THE LENGTH PRECONDITION: do not run it on a 7829-word report. If it is ever run, it must be stacked with a padding purge (C6) so total words stay at/below the reference, otherwise it is a re-run of the Rank7/Rank9 density-for-length trade the team already measured as a loss.

5. PRESERVE THE INSTRUMENT: if the arm is armed but not rendered, multi_section_generator.py:11986-11988 still adds synthesis words to `total_words`. Any experiment must report BODY words from report.md, not `compose_summary.total_words`, or the length metric is silently corrupted."
   },
   {
    "refuted": true,
    "reasoning": "REFUTED on four independent grounds; two are demonstrated by running the code.

1) THE CLAIM IS EXACTLY THE NOISE FLOOR — BY ITS OWN ARITHMETIC.
Weights (verified, /home/polaris/polaris_project/race_task72_criteria.txt): insight 0.32, comprehensiveness 0.29, instruction-following 0.25, readability 0.14. Overall is the plain weighted sum — I checked Rank10: 0.32*0.4238 + 0.29*0.4549 + 0.25*0.4409 + 0.14*0.3774 = 0.4306 vs measured 0.4313. So the proposal's insight 0.4238 -> 0.475 gives dOverall = 0.32 * 0.0512 = +0.0164. The measured noise floor is +/-0.016 (Rank9 vs Rank10 are the SAME effective config and scored 0.4155 / 0.4313). The claimed range +1.0..+2.0 pts = 0.63x..1.25x the noise floor. At n=1 the effect is by construction indistinguishable from judge variance. This also violates the team's own pre-registered rule (FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md 2026-07-13 03:00: "EVERY lever in both plans is SMALLER THAN OUR MEASUREMENT NOISE... score 5x paired, SE ~0.008, ship iff delta >= +0.010"). The proposal is presented as a free n=1 env flip.

Worse, the required insight jump (+0.051) exceeds anything ever measured on that dimension here:
 - baseline -> Rank10: insight +0.040, and it cost a 2.75x length increase (3,343 -> 9,194 words); net overall only +0.026 (~1.6 noise widths).
 - Rank7 (long sections): insight +0.023 but overall -0.001 — readability collapsed 0.4008 -> 0.3379 and ate the whole gain.
 - STEP-3 insight directive: insight -0.040 (0.4293 -> 0.3890), overall -0.035 (2.2x noise, NEGATIVE).
An appended section producing +0.051 insight has no precedent in this corpus.

2) IT IS NOT MEANINGFULLY DIFFERENT FROM THE STEP-3 INSIGHT DIRECTIVE — AND ITS GATE SELECTS FOR THE FAILURE REGISTER.
The content is produced by ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT (analyst_synthesis.py:71+): an LLM told to "write a longer interpretive narrative", "hedge appropriately", "4-6 sub-sections", "1500-3000 words". That is the same intervention class that returned -0.040. The downstream gate can only SUBTRACT insight, never add it. And it subtracts in exactly the wrong direction: _is_disclosable_interpretation (analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:264-302) makes an _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE hedge cue MANDATORY on every uncited interpretive sentence and FORBIDS any decimal, the word "percent", and any _QUANT_CLAIM_RE quantitative claim. So the section's uncited interpretive spine is, by construction, hedge-saturated and number-free. The RACE insight rubric rewards the opposite: "Analytical Depth ... deeply analyze the underlying mechanisms" (w=0.25) and "Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation ... areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty" (w=0.25) — these require taking positions on contested, specific findings. Mandatory hedging is structurally anti-thesis, and the SOTA brief's own defect #6 names the missing thing as "a thesis". The proposal admits this as failure mode (c) and then discounts it without evidence.

3) DEMONSTRATED DEFECT A — A DROPPED SENTENCE DELETES THE NEXT SECTION HEADER (structural corruption, unanticipated).
I ran screen_synthesis_against_baskets with PROMOTE+BASKET+DISCLOSED on. split_into_sentences (provenance_generator.py:3002) splits only on [.!?]+ws+Capital, so a markdown "### Header" (no terminal punctuation) is NEVER a split boundary: the splitter glues "[last sentence of para A]\n\n### Header\n\n[first sentence of para B]" into ONE unit, which becomes ONE keep/drop decision in the promote branch (:689-722). Observed output:
  DROPPED: ['Employment in exposed roles fell by 27.4% ... [2].\n\n### Comparative Considerations Across Industries\n\nBroadly, exposure appears concentrated in high-wage cognitive occupations [2].']
One ungrounded number deleted a section HEADING plus a grounded sentence. Consequence: the 4-6 subheading structure the system prompt demands gets shredded in proportion to the drop rate (box2 measured 79/81 drops under promote; basket mode still drops any sentence whose number is not in the cited union). This hits readability S1 (structure/headings, w=0.20), S2 (paragraph cohesion, 0.15) and F1 (formatting, 0.10) = 45% of the readability dimension. Corollary: the header branch of _is_disclosable_interpretation (`raw.startswith("#")`, :275) is effectively DEAD CODE — a real header is almost never the start of a split unit.

4) DEMONSTRATED DEFECT B — THE PROPOSAL'S ONE CODE CHANGE DOES NOT DO WHAT IT CLAIMS.
The rewrite targets ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE (analyst_synthesis.py:64-69) to remove "not individually span-verified ... not audit-grade claims". But PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN (default ON — which the proposal explicitly RELIES on to strip inline markers) REMOVES that disclosure from the text and INSERTS a second, hardcoded, untouched note at the top of the section (_render_clean_synthesis, analyst_synthesis.py:~627-635). Actual shipped output from my run:
  "_Confidence labels for this interpretive section: 1 moderate, 1 no-source-found. Each was a per-sentence groundedness check, summarized here; the whole section is interpretive and not individually span-verified._"
So the exact self-indicting language survives verbatim, PLUS a machine-generated count of NO-SOURCE sentences, at the head of a literature review whose instruction-following criteria (w=0.25) include "Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles'" and "Adherence to 'Literature Review' Format ... rather than ... an opinion piece". The proposal's stated mechanism for avoiding a self-indictment is broken.

5) THE LENGTH TAX IS A KNOWN, CLOSED NEGATIVE, AND C1 SHIPS IT STANDALONE.
Human reference = 9,029 words; Rank10 = 9,194. SOTA_REVIEW_BRIEF: "We are already AT reference length. More words cannot help. Anything that trades insight-per-word for length is now strictly harmful." The section targets 1,500-3,000 words -> 10,700-12,200 total (19-35% over reference). The proposal admits this (failure mode (a)) and defers the remedy to C6, which is not part of C1. Rank7's precedent for a length push is readability -0.063 = -0.88 pts at w=0.14 — over half the claimed gain, from one dimension alone.

6) SCOPE / FRAMING PROBLEMS.
"Zero new code" is false in the proposal's own text (it carries a code change), and that code change is inert (see 4). The flags are not "off by accident": the emitter guard (analyst_synthesis.py:685-695) refuses the layer because un-dropped synthesis is "the exact ungated posture that was banned", and the guard is default-ON so "only an explicit operator opt-out disables the guard" — this is an operator policy lock, not a dark lane. Finally, the report's own preamble still promises "Every quantitative claim is span-grounded to a cited source; claims that could not be verified ... were removed rather than paraphrased." Appending a section that advertises N no-source sentences contradicts the report's own contract inside the same document — and the disclosure rewrite makes that worse, not better, by deleting the honest signal while render-clean still prints the unsourced count.

7) EXPECTED VALUE IS BELOW THE POINT ESTIMATE. The reasoning-first writer already returned content="" in drb_72 (#1323/#1335 dark-synthesis trap) and the emitter fail-closes to ("",0,0). Combined with the promote-mode drop rate, the modal outcome is a small/empty section plus shredded headers — a burned generate+score cycle whose delta is inside noise in either direction.

Bottom line: the claimed gain is at the noise floor by the proposal's own arithmetic, the only measured precedent for this intervention class is a 2.5x-noise NEGATIVE, and flipping the flags today ships two reproduced defects (header-shredding drops; a self-indicting confidence note the code change fails to remove).",
    "fix": "Do NOT flip the flags as proposed. If this lane is to be tested at all, it needs four things first, none of which are "zero code":

(a) FIX THE SPLIT/DROP UNIT so a markdown header can never sit inside a keep/drop unit. In analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.screen_synthesis_against_baskets, pre-segment the synthesis on line boundaries (headers/blank lines) BEFORE calling split_into_sentences, and pass header lines through untouched. Repro: a single ungrounded number currently deletes the following "### ..." heading (see /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:689-722 with provenance_generator.py:3002).

(b) SUPPRESS OR REWRITE THE RENDER-CLEAN NOTE, not just ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE. The hardcoded note in _render_clean_synthesis (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/analyst_synthesis.py, `note = "_Confidence labels for this interpretive section: ..."`) still ships "not individually span-verified" plus a count of no-source sentences. The proposed PG_SYNTH_DISCLOSURE_SCHOLARLY flag does not touch it.

(c) HOLD TOTAL WORDS CONSTANT. Do not run C1 standalone at 10,700-12,200 words against a 9,029-word reference. Either bundle the padding purge (C6) or cap the synthesis section so total words stay within +/-5% of Rank10 — making this an insight-per-word test rather than a re-run of the Rank7 length failure.

(d) MEASURE IT LEGALLY. The claim (+1.6 pts) equals the +/-0.016 noise floor. Score 5x paired against Rank10 per the team's own pre-registered rule (SE ~0.008, ship iff delta >= +0.010). An n=1 arm cannot resolve this effect and should not be run.

Cheaper alternative that targets the same rubric line without the length tax: the insight criteria reward mechanism analysis and critical synthesis of CONTESTED SPECIFICS. Rewriting existing verified body prose to contrast competing numeric findings across sources (at zero added words) attacks "Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation" (w=0.25 of insight) directly, whereas this lane's gate mandates hedging and BANS numbers in exactly the sentences meant to carry the interpretation."
   }
  ]
 },
 {
  "change": "Body-level INFERENCE tier in the production verifier",
  "id": "C2",
  "survives": false,
  "refuted_votes": "3/3",
  "objections": [
   {
    "refuted": true,
    "reasoning": "REFUTED on the hallucination lens. The proposal's safety rests on three deterministic floors; reading the actual code, two do not exist as described and the third is blind to the novel content class.

(1) NEUTRAL IS the ungrounded verdict. The tier converts "span does not support this claim" into a keep. Every deterministic leg cited as the backstop (number_not_in_any_cited_span, percent_not_in_cited_span, no_integer_overlap, binding_qualifier_dropped, provenance_generator.py:2266-2400) is NUMERIC. None can inspect a relation 

Produce the FINAL BUILD PLAN. Rules:
- DROP anything refuted unless you can rebut with code evidence (quote file:line).
- The faithfulness contract must be AIRTIGHT. If the skeptics found a hallucination path, close it explicitly or drop the tier.

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

content.content:
TASK 72 PROMPT:
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

DIMENSION WEIGHTS: {"readability": 0.14, "insight": 0.32, "comprehensiveness": 0.29, "instruction_following": 0.25}

=== COMPREHENSIVENESS (weight 0.29) ===
- [w=0.10] Grounding in AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Assesses whether the review adequately defines AI within the context of the 4IR and explains its fundamental role as a driver of labor market restructuring. This contextual framing is crucial for understanding the scope and nature of the impacts discussed.
- [w=0.25] Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered :: Evaluates the extent to which the review addresses the multifaceted nature of AI's impact on the labor market, including job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes.
- [w=0.25] Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis :: Checks if the review examines the restructuring impact of AI across a diverse range of relevant industries, highlighting both common trends and sector-specific nuances. The task explicitly requires focusing on 'various industries.'
- [w=0.15] Exploration of AI's Disruptive Character and Scale :: Assesses whether the review sufficiently explores the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI, including the magnitude, speed, and transformative potential of these changes on labor market structures and dynamics.
- [w=0.15] Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: Evaluates if the review synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles), covering the main research themes, findings, and ongoing debates regarding AI's impact on the labor market. This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
- [w=0.10] Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts :: Assesses whether the review presents a nuanced perspective by discussing both the challenges (e.g., displacement, skill gaps, inequality) and opportunities (e.g., new job creation, productivity gains, enhanced work quality) arising from AI's influence on the labor market.

=== INSIGHT (weight 0.32) ===
- [w=0.25] Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Market Restructuring Mechanisms :: Assesses if the review moves beyond superficial descriptions of labor market changes to deeply analyze the underlying *mechanisms* (e.g., task automation, skill augmentation, job creation/destruction dynamics, organizational adaptation) through which AI specifically reshapes job roles, skill requirements, and overall labor market structures.
- [w=0.25] Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: Evaluates the review's ability to critically synthesize diverse findings from high-quality journal articles to present a nuanced understanding of AI's 'significant disruptions.' This includes identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects.
- [w=0.15] Insightful Integration of AI's Role within the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Measures how effectively the review articulates and leverages the 4IR framework to provide a deeper, more contextualized understanding of the *nature, scale, and interconnectedness* of AI's transformative power on the labor market, rather than treating AI's impact in isolation or merely mentioning the 4IR superficially.
- [w=0.20] Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives :: Assesses whether the review transcends simple summarization to identify and clearly articulate overarching emergent themes, significant theoretical linkages between concepts, or potentially novel perspectives derived from the synthesis of the reviewed literature on AI's labor market impact.
- [w=0.15] Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research Agendas :: Evaluates the review's capacity to distill strategically valuable implications (e.g., for policy, education, workforce adaptation strategies) and to clearly delineate critical research gaps or promising future research agendas based on the comprehensive synthesis of the current state of knowledge.

=== INSTRUCTION_FOLLOWING (weight 0.25) ===
- [w=0.10] Adherence to 'Literature Review' Format and Purpose :: Assesses if the article is structured and executed as a literature review, meaning it synthesizes and discusses existing published research on the topic, rather than presenting new empirical findings or being solely an opinion piece.
- [w=0.20] Consistent Focus on 'Restructuring Impact of AI on the Labor Market' :: Evaluates whether the article's content remains consistently and primarily focused on AI's restructuring effects on the labor market, as per the core task instruction, without significant digressions into unrelated AI applications or general economic theory not tied to labor impacts.
- [w=0.15] Integration of 'AI as a Key Driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution' Theme :: Checks if the review explicitly incorporates and discusses AI within the specific context of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, as mandated by the task's focus requirements.
- [w=0.15] Explicit Addressal of AI-Driven 'Significant Disruptions' in the Labor Market :: Assesses whether the review specifically addresses and elaborates on the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI in the labor market, fulfilling another key focus point of the task.
- [w=0.15] Coverage of AI's Impact on 'Various Industries' :: Evaluates if the review discusses the impact of AI on the labor market across a spectrum of 'various industries,' rather than limiting its scope to a single industry or providing only a generic overview, as per the instruction.
- [w=0.15] Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' :: Verifies that all cited sources are academic, peer-reviewed journal articles (or meet a clear standard of 'high-quality' if defined contextually, e.g., leading journals), and that other forms of literature (e.g., books, conference proceedings, news articles, blogs, non-peer-reviewed reports) are not cited, adhering to the 'only cites high-quality... journal articles' constraint.
- [w=0.10] Exclusive Citation of 'English-Language' Journal Articles :: Ensures that all journal articles cited in the review are originally published in English or are established English translations, strictly adhering to the language constraint for sources.

=== READABILITY (weight 0.14) ===
- [w=0.20] L1: Language Clarity, Precision, and Academic Tone :: Evaluates the accuracy and fluency of sentence construction, grammar, spelling, and punctuation. Assesses the precise, consistent, and appropriate use of technical terminology related to AI, labor markets, the Fourth Industrial Revolution, and specific industries. Ensures an objective, formal academic tone suitable for a literature review. For example, terms like 'skill-biased technical change' or specific AI techniques should be used correctly and, if necessary, briefly contextualized for a broader academic audience.
- [w=0.20] S1: Overall Structure and Logical Organization :: Assesses the coherence and logic of the review's macro-structure. This includes a clear introduction defining the review's scope, purpose, and organizational map; logically sequenced thematic sections with informative headings that reflect the facets of AI's impact on labor (e.g., by industry, skill level, type of disruption); and a conclusion that effectively synthesizes the main themes from the literature and perhaps points to gaps or future research directions.
- [w=0.15] S2: Paragraph Cohesion and Transitions :: Evaluates if each paragraph focuses on a single, clear idea or theme derived from the literature, with sentences flowing logically within the paragraph. Assesses the effectiveness of transitional phrases, sentences, and logical connectors in linking ideas smoothly between paragraphs and across different sections of the review, ensuring a coherent narrative thread.
- [w=0.15] P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information :: Assesses how clearly, concisely, and accurately key arguments, findings, theories, and methodologies from the cited journal articles are presented. Evaluates the effectiveness of synthesizing information from multiple sources to build a coherent understanding of topics (e.g., AI's effect on job displacement vs. creation, or changes in skill demands across industries), rather than just serially summarizing individual papers. Checks for appropriate information density and avoidance of redundancy.
- [w=0.10] D1: Clarity of Data/Evidence Referenced or Summarized :: Evaluates the clarity with which quantitative or qualitative data, empirical findings, or specific evidence from cited studies (e.g., statistics on automation rates, survey results on worker sentiment) are described, interpreted, or summarized within the review. If the review itself uses tables or figures to compile or synthesize information from sources (e.g., a summary table of key studies and their findings), assesses their clarity, labeling, and effectiveness in aiding comprehension.
- [w=0.10] F1: Formatting, Layout, and Visual Consistency :: Evaluates the use of consistent and professional formatting (e.g., font choice and size, spacing, margins, heading styles) that enhances readability. Assesses the overall visual layout for ease of navigation and reduced reader fatigue, including clear paragraph breaks. Also considers the visual consistency and unobtrusive integration of in-text citations and the clarity of the bibliography's formatting, which contributes to the academic presentation.
- [w=0.10] A1: Audience Adaptation and Explanation of Terms :: Assesses if the language, level of detail, and style of argumentation are appropriate for an academic audience interested in AI's impact on the labor market. Evaluates whether potentially ambiguous or highly specialized terms are sufficiently contextualized or briefly explained to ensure understanding by readers who may not be experts in every specific sub-domain discussed (e.g., specific AI algorithms or niche economic theories).

input.description: Read SOTA brief and benchmark target

content.content:
# BRIEF: get POLARIS to SOTA on DeepResearch Bench (RACE), task 72

## What RACE actually is (read this first — most of our past reasoning got it wrong)
RACE is REFERENCE-BASED. An LLM judge scores BOTH our report and a human REFERENCE report
against per-task weighted criteria, then:  **Overall = target / (target + reference)**.
- **0.500 = TIED with the human reference.**  Below 0.5 = worse than the reference.
- Judge noise measured on this box: **±0.016** (two identical-config runs scored 0.4155 and 0.4313).
- EVALUATOR: the benchmark switched judges in 2026 (Gemini-2.5-Pro deprecated -> **GPT-5.5**). Our sweep
  used `openai/gpt-5.5`, i.e. the CURRENT evaluator. Our numbers are on the right scale.

## THE TARGET: 0.5265 (SOTA). WE ARE AT 0.4313. THE GAP IS 9.5 POINTS.
| System | Overall |
|---|---|
| **ADORE (rank 1, SOTA)** | **0.5265** |
| Tavily Research | 0.5244 |
| (human reference = parity) | 0.5000 |
| Gemini-2.5-Pro DR (the ORIGINAL paper's number — now STALE) | 0.4888 |
| **POLARIS Rank10 (us, best)** | **0.4313** |
| POLARIS baseline | 0.4052 |
**The top systems have BEATEN the human reference (>0.50).** Beating the reference is NOT enough for SOTA
any more — we must beat it by ~2.7 points. Do NOT plan to 0.4888; that target is a year out of date.
CAVEAT the plan must respect: leaderboard scores are averaged over ALL 100 benchmark tasks; ours is ONE
task (#72). A plan that overfits to task 72 wins nothing. Prefer levers that generalise across tasks.

## The scoreboard (measured tonight, official harness, task 72)
| Arm | Words | Overall | Comprehens. | Insight | Instr-Follow | Readability |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CTRL baseline | 3,343 | 0.4052 | 0.4310 | 0.3839 | 0.4032 | 0.4008 |
| Rank7 (long sections) | 7,615 | 0.4041 | 0.4326 | 0.4070 | 0.3996 | 0.3379 |
| Rank8 (menu cap only) | 4,226 | 0.3957 | 0.4197 | 0.3903 | 0.3907 | 0.3634 |
| Rank9 (both levers) | 7,937 | 0.4155 | 0.4372 | 0.4122 | 0.4241 | 0.3588 |
| **Rank10 (best)** | **9,194** | **0.4313** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | 0.3774 |
Rank9 and Rank10 are the SAME effective config (the theme floor was a no-op) => their 0.0158 gap IS the noise floor.

## DIMENSION WEIGHTS FOR TASK 72 (this is where points live)
**Insight 0.32 | Comprehensiveness 0.29 | Instruction-Following 0.25 | Readability 0.14**
Insight is the single biggest lever. Readability is the only dimension we made WORSE, but it is the
LIGHTEST weight — do not over-invest there.

## THE LENGTH QUESTION IS CLOSED
The human REFERENCE report is **9,029 words**. Rank10 is **9,194 words**. We are already AT reference
length. More words cannot help. Anything that trades insight-per-word for length is now strictly harmful.

## THE TASK PROMPT (verbatim — every instruction here is graded)
"Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the
labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing
significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality,
English-language journal articles."

## KNOWN, UNFIXED DEFECTS (found by Fable's prose gate, all still shipping)
1. **4IR framing is SHALLOW, not absent.** (CORRECTED — verify claims like this yourself, do not trust this brief.)
   The earlier "zero mentions" finding was about RANK7 and is now STALE. Measured in the RANK10 BODY:
   "Fourth Industrial Revolution" x4 in 7,742 body words, "4IR" x0. (A naive whole-file grep says x8 — the
   other 4 are in the BIBLIOGRAPHY. This body-vs-references trap already fooled our own density instrument
   once tonight. Split the body from the `## References` section before counting ANYTHING.)
   All 4 body mentions are NAME-DROPS — 4IR appears as scenery, never as an organising frame. The criteria
   (instruction-following w=0.15 "Integration of AI as a Key Driver of the 4IR"; comprehensiveness w=0.10
   "Grounding in the 4IR Context") demand the review DISCUSS AI *within* that context. So the fix is
   INTEGRATION, not insertion. Compare against how the REFERENCE report frames 4IR — it opens with it.
2. **We cite non-journal sources.** Instruction-following criterion w=0.15 demands "all cited sources are
   academic, peer-reviewed journal articles ... books, conference proceedings, news articles, blogs,
   non-peer-reviewed reports are NOT cited." We ship Google Scholar PROFILE PAGES, mirror URLs, and reports.
   NOTE: the driver KILLED the source-eligibility firewall because it did not improve RELEVANCE — but RACE
   grades source type as INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING (0.25 weight), not relevance. It was judged against the wrong yardstick.
3. **Readability regressions that literally ship:** 27 stray `(also mirrored)` markers leak into prose; TWO
   sections begin MID-WORD ("ings in the last two years.[85]"); the fact_dedup pass writes its own internal
   cross-references into the report body ("...is detailed under Productivity Gains from Generative AI at Work.").
4. **~30-35% of the added words are padding**: verbatim cross-section duplicate facts; the SAME fact cited to
   two different sources (invisible to every dedup we have); and VACUOUS metadata-as-content sentences that pass
   strict_verify because it checks GROUNDING, not INFORMATIVENESS — e.g. "The study received 239 Scopus
   citations.[22]", "This dataset was published in the Strategic Management Journal, volume 42, issue 12."
5. **Rule #9 (15 distinct sources per section) is UNSATISFIABLE and silently ignored** — met in 1 of 10 sections.
   When a prompt rule is unsatisfiable the model does not fail loudly, it PADS by restating what it has.
   One section spends 28 sentences on 8 refs, TEN of them citing [1] alone.
6. **Insight is our LOWEST-scoring dimension and the HIGHEST-weighted (0.32).** strict_verify guarantees every
   sentence is GROUNDED but nothing in the pipeline rewards SYNTHESIS across sources, contrast of competing
   findings, or a thesis. The reference report synthesises; we serially summarise.

## YOUR JOB
Read the FULL Rank11 report **LINE BY LINE, END TO END — every line, no sampling, no skimming, no
"representative excerpts"**. Then read the FULL human REFERENCE report the same way. The reference is the
thing we are scored against: the single most valuable output you can produce is a precise account of what
the reference DOES that we DO NOT.
Then produce a CONCRETE, ORDERED plan to reach **SOTA = 0.5265** from our 0.4313 — a 9.5-point climb.
For EVERY proposed change state: (a) which DIMENSION and which WEIGHTED CRITERION it targets, (b) the
expected point gain and your reasoning, (c) how it could FAIL or backfire, (d) how we would MEASURE it
against a ±0.016 noise floor. Rank by points-per-unit-effort. Be brutally honest: if a lever is exhausted,
say so. We have optimised proxies (words, verified sentences, density) all night that turned out NOT to
move the score — do not hand us another proxy.
====
# POLARIS BENCHMARK TARGET — the real scoreboard (from web search 2026-07-11)

## What we must beat
**FS-Researcher** (arXiv:2602.01566, ACL 2026) — "Test-Time Scaling for Long-Horizon Research
Tasks with File-System-Based Agents". A file-system dual-agent (Context Builder + Report Writer)
that scales research BEYOND the context window via a persistent knowledge base. This is the same
thesis as POLARIS (process ALL 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count), not one context window). It claims **SOTA report
quality** across backbones. Code: github.com/Ignoramus0817/FS-Researcher.

## The benchmarks it is scored on (both open-ended report quality)
1. **DeepResearch Bench** (arXiv:2506.11763) — 100 PhD-level research tasks.
   - Scorer: **RACE** — reference-based LLM judge, 4 dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights:
     Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability.
     Compares the target report against a high-quality REFERENCE report.
   - Download: `github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench` — tasks at
     `data/prompt_data/query.jsonl` (100 tasks); HF dataset `muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Dataset`.
   - Leaderboard (ChatGPT/Gemini deep-research RACE scores) is on HuggingFace.
2. **DeepConsult** — open-ended consulting-style report quality (pairwise/quality judge).
3. (also referenced for the agent side: BrowseComp, BFCL, TAU-Bench, MCP Agent Bench.)

Related: **DeepResearch Bench II** (arXiv:2601.08536) — rubric-from-expert-report diagnosis
(the DRB-II wrapper already stubbed in the compete wheel).

## What this means for POLARIS
- **RACE is the correct instrument** — confirms the compete wheel's direction; DeepTRACE (faithfulness)
  is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.
- "Beat FS-Researcher" is now RUNNABLE: run POLARIS on the DeepResearch Bench 100 tasks, score with
  RACE against the reference reports, compare to FS-Researcher + the ChatGPT/Gemini leaderboard.
- The two build wheels serve this end:
  - **OUTLINE** → depth + compute-and-prove drives Comprehensiveness + Insight (RACE dims 1-2) and
    the faithfulness moat (DeepTRACE) that frontier products lack.
  - **COMPOSE** → renders all 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count) fast + faithful so nothing is dropped for latency =
    Comprehensiveness + Readability, at full corpus depth.
- Open gap: we still have NO FS-Researcher report artifact locally; the benchmark download closes it
  (we can run POLARIS on the same 100 tasks and score head-to-head).

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Score a composed POLARIS report with RACE (DeepResearch Bench) against a benchmark task.

RACE (third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py) is REFERENCE-BASED: it matches
the target article, the reference article, and the per-task criteria by EXACT prompt string, then
an LLM judge (openai/gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter) scores both target and reference on the task's
dynamic-weighted dimensions. Overall = target/(target+reference) (0.5 == parity with reference).

This wrapper: (1) reads a report.md, (2) writes it as the target jsonl under the benchmark task's
EXACT prompt (so ref+criteria align), (3) invokes the official RACE harness limited to that task,
(4) prints results/race/<model>/race_result.txt.

Usage:
    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
    python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/live_compose/run2/report.md \
        --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
DRB = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench"


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True, help="path to report.md")
    ap.add_argument("--task-id", default="72")
    ap.add_argument("--model-name", default="polaris_task72")
    ap.add_argument("--race-model", default=os.getenv("RACE_MODEL", "openai/gpt-5.5"))
    ap.add_argument("--max-workers", type=int, default=4)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
        print("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set — source .env first", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    report_text = Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    # Find the benchmark task's EXACT prompt (target/ref/criteria all key on it).
    task = None
    for line in (DRB / "data/prompt_data/query.jsonl").read_text().splitlines():
        o = json.loads(line)
        if str(o["id"]) == str(args.task_id):
            task = o
            break
    if task is None:
        print(f"BLOCKED: task id {args.task_id} not in query.jsonl", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    prompt = task["prompt"]
    lang = task.get("language", "en")

    # Single-task query file so ONLY this task is scored.
    q_path = DRB / f"data/prompt_data/query_task{args.task_id}.jsonl"
    q_path.write_text(json.dumps(task, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

input.thesis: C1 and C2 are DEAD and stay dead — no rebuttal survives contact with the code. I verified the C1 kill shot myself: the scored composer's report assembly (scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:652-668) renders only sr.verified_text + Limitations + References; analyst_synthesis_text never reaches report.md, so the env-only flip is mechanically score-invisible while arming ~100 discarded LLM calls and corrupting total_words. C2's NEUTRAL-keep converts 'span does not support this claim' into a keep with only numeric deterministic backstops, which is the hallucination hole our own pre-registration declared unshippable. The thesis that DOES survive — endorsed independently by all three skeptics as the 'cheaper alternative' — is the pre-registered bundled arm: rebuild the DOCUMENT (H3s, 150-word topic-sentence paragraphs, study tables, bullets) and weave interpretation OVER frozen verified contrasts, at zero added words, implemented as an offline rewrite stage on the banked Rank10 artifact so the whole wave needs ZERO compose runs and is scored 5x-paired against a k=5-pinned baseline. Faithfulness moves from 'one tier with a soft verdict' (refuted) to 'disjoint sentence classes with deterministic membership': facts never bypass the frozen verifier, interpretation is deterministically barred from carrying any checkable factual atom, and everything else fail-closes.
input.faithfulness_contract: Every sentence in the shipped report belongs to exactly ONE class, decided by deterministic checks that run before and independent of any LLM, fail-closed at paragraph granularity (any unclassifiable sentence reverts its whole paragraph to the source text). CLASS F (FACT): either byte-identical to an already-verified sentence (the reflow validator asserts the multiset of citation-bearing sentences is preserved), or a rewritten/fused sentence that re-passes the FULL frozen verify_sentence_provenance gate (provenance_generator.py:2098 — all deterministic numeric legs + entailment) against the union of its parent sentences' cited rows (spans from bibliography.json direct_quotes + corpus); NEUTRAL or any verifier failure = revert to the parent sentences, never keep, never drop content. Only class F may carry [n] citations or numbers. CLASS S (STRUCTURE): headers, transitions, and table cells assembled deterministically from sidecar metadata and verified-sentence substrings — zero LLM-originated factual content by construction. CLASS I (INTERPRETATION — the only new admitted content) is legal iff ALL of: (1) contains no digits, percents, units, or comparative magnitudes; (2) every proper noun / capitalized multi-token span already appears verbatim in the same paragraph's class-F sentences or the task-prompt concept whitelist — this kills fabricated attribution including the single-name/org gap the skeptics found in _AUTHOR_ATTR_RE; (3) carries no citation marker, so it can never masquerade as verified; (4) is adjacent to ≥2 class-F sentences citing ≥2 distinct sources and shares ≥2 content lemmas with them — it must interpret the compared findings, not free-float; (5) uses an epistemic frame and no universal/predictive-certainty pattern; (6) passes a MANDATORY contradiction screen — the existing sentinel judge with the paragraph's class-F sentences as premise, CONTRADICTED = dropped, judge error/timeout = dropped. This is kill-on-contradiction, NOT the refuted keep-on-NEUTRAL: because class I is deterministically barred from carrying any checkable factual atom, NEUTRAL here means 'a hedged relational gloss the premises neither entail nor deny', not 'an ungrounded fact'; (7) volume caps: ≤1 per paragraph, ≤8% of body sentences, and EVERY kept class-I sentence is enumerated verbatim with its premises in the audit sidecar (closing the count-only audit gap the skeptics documented) and manually read before any score is trusted. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, unconditionally: any new number outside the frozen verifier; any new named actor; any [n] on unverified text; anything a judge calls contradicted; everything when the judge is unavailable; any LLM paraphrase of a class-F sentence that fails re-verification. GUARANTEE: the only wrong-but-shipped content possible is an explicitly hedged, uncited, volume-capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss over correct verified facts — no fabricated fact, number, quote, or attribution can ship, because those are excluded by deterministic string-level checks, not by model judgment. The skeptics' four concrete attacks (polarity inversion of the 14% finding, Goldman-Sachs attribution, cross-source number transplant, 'replace all lawyers') are encoded as permanent regression tests that must all DROP before any judge call is spent. The report preamble's verification claim (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:644-651) is amended, not softened: it additionally discloses that connective interpretive sentences carry no citations and no quantitative claims.
input.expected_gain: Honest accounting from the TRUE baseline ~0.42 (Rank9/10/11 mean; quoting Rank10's 0.4313 is the luckiest draw): W1 structure +0.005-0.012, W2 interpretive weave +0.006-0.016, W3 tables +0.001-0.004, W4 confession purge +0.001-0.003, W5 4IR framing +0.003-0.008. Bundle point estimate +0.02 to +0.04 overall → ~0.44-0.46. This DOES NOT reach 0.5265 — it falls ~0.07-0.09 short, and saying so is part of the plan. The residual lives in three places wave 1 deliberately does not touch: (a) comprehensiveness 'Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis' (w=0.25 of 0.29) — we have ZERO industry-organized content, and fixing it means regrouping evidence into a sectoral section at the outline seam, a compose-level change; (b) IF 'Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles' (w=0.15 of 0.25) — post-ArticleCleaner the ONLY scoring path is venue names written into running prose, deterministically checkable against row metadata; (c) the deep one: the reference converts evidence into an ARGUMENT — a wave 2 would have to rebuild composition around comparison-matrix bundles (normalize by technology/unit-of-analysis/outcome/horizon, write from bundles not source cards) so synthesis is the unit of generation, not a post-hoc weave. Even a successful wave 2 honestly projects ~0.47-0.50; the last ~0.03 to ADORE's 0.5265 (a 100-task mean vs our n=1 task, so the comparison itself is soft) has no evidenced lever tonight. If wave 1's paired delta comes back <+0.008, even that ladder is dead and the honest report is 'task-72 RACE is insensitive to everything we can faithfully change.'
input.kill_rule: PRE-REGISTERED BEFORE ANY SCORING, in order: (0) INSTRUMENT GATE — W0's k=5 identical-artifact rescore first; if per-call SD > 0.020, STOP all scoring-driven work (no lever we own is resolvable) and fix the instrument before touching code. (1) MECHANISM GATE — no judge call is spent until every W1-W5 deterministic test is green on the CLEANED artifact and I have read 100% of the enumerated class-I sentences; the four skeptic attack cases must all DROP. (2) SHIP GATE — the bundle ships iff 5x-paired mean delta vs the pinned Rank10 baseline ≥ +0.010 (SE ~0.008). (3) DEATH GATE — if paired delta < +0.008, the structure+prose-mode thesis is DEAD ON THIS TASK: no variant iteration, no wave 2 built on it, write the negative result and escalate to the operator with the two remaining options (argument-first composer rebuild, or accept ~0.43 and redirect effort to the 100-task generalization where task-72 overfitting is worthless anyway). (4) FAITHFULNESS TRUMP — if ANY shipped sentence is found (regression test, audit read, or spot check) to carry a fabricated fact, number, or attribution, class I is stripped permanently regardless of score and the arm re-runs structure-only; the moat is not for sale. (5) SUNK-COST BOUND — wave 1 is ≤1 build-day and judge-calls-only (zero 65-minute composes; the entire arm runs on the banked outputs/rank10_sections_compose artifact); if the build itself exceeds that, the complexity is the warning and we stop and reassess. C1 and C2 remain dropped permanently unless someone produces code, not prose, that closes every enumerated hole.
